codeReview.summary: Comprehensive implementation of AI-powered failure recovery flow. The feature correctly implements all requirements: automatic recovery trigger on failed/cancelled/waitlisted courses, dependent course analysis, alternative suggestions from articulation data with prerequisite validation, and waitlisted-specific messaging. Code quality is high with proper separation of concerns between utilities, components, and API routes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The recovery-analysis.ts utility follows a functional approach with pure functions for analyzing plan data, which aligns well with the architecture.md's data flow descriptions. The utility is well-tested (19 tests) and covers edge cases.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/utils/recovery-analysis.ts: Implements findDependentCourses, findCompletedCourses, arePrerequisitesSatisfied, findEarliestValidSemester, and analyzeRecovery functions with clear interfaces

sharedStateObservations.observation: The RecoveryMessage component uses a non-color indicator pattern (icons: ✗, ✕, ⏳) for different statuses, which aligns with accessibility requirements from the feature description. This pattern should be documented as a convention for status indicators.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/recovery-message.tsx: Lines 44-46 use emoji icons as non-color indicators for failed/cancelled/waitlisted statuses

sharedStateObservations.observation: The chat API route (route.ts) implements a sophisticated system prompt builder that conditionally appends recovery context. This pattern of building dynamic system prompts based on context could be useful for future features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/app/api/chat/route.ts: Lines 189-280 buildRecoveryPrompt function and conditional prompt appending at line 298

sharedStateObservations.observation: The component exports RecoveryAlternative interface separately to allow parent components to use it in their callbacks. This is a good pattern for type sharing across component boundaries.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/recovery-message.tsx: Line 3 exports RecoveryAlternative interface, used by plan-detail-client.tsx

sharedStateObservations.observation: The accept-alternative API route uses 'as never' type assertions when inserting to Supabase, which is a pattern used throughout the codebase for type safety with Supabase's strict typing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/app/api/plan/[id]/accept-alternative/route.ts: Lines 122, 149, 159 use 'as never' for Supabase inserts/updates

sharedStateObservations.observation: The recovery-analysis tests cover all utility functions with comprehensive edge cases including empty arrays, multiple dependents, prerequisite satisfaction with completed courses, and waitlisted status handling.
The RecoveryMessage component tests use vi.fn() for mocking callbacks and test all UI states including loading, success, and empty states. Good coverage of accessibility text content.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/recovery-message.test.tsx: 12 tests including waitlisted messaging and counselor guidance verification

summary: The ai-recovery-flow feature is well-implemented and passes review. All requirements are met: (1) automatic trigger on failed/cancelled/waitlisted courses, (2) dependent course enumeration with prerequisite analysis, (3) alternative suggestions from articulation data with code/title/reasoning, (4) prerequisite satisfaction validation, (5) plan updates on alternative acceptance, (6) waitlisted-specific messaging acknowledging uncertainty, (7) clear guidance when no alternatives exist, (8) completed courses respected. The implementation includes 31 tests (19 utility + 12 component) and follows project conventions. No blocking issues found.
